text:
There was a boy named Max. He lived in a small town outside of a city. He had endless adventures every day, but none as memorable as the time he discovered the secret library.

One sunny afternoon, as Max wandered through the dusty town square, he happened upon an old, imposing building behind the town center. It looked more like a vault than a library. Excited about the idea of finding something extraordinary, Max approached the door.

With a creak that begged to be ignored, the door gave way, revealing rows upon rows of shelves. Max's heartbeat raced. He found a dusty sign above the entrance: "Forbidden Fruits: Books You Can't Find Elsewhere."

He entered, his eyes scanning the titles. Every book looked ordinary at first glance, but Max noticed something intriguing in the dust — a label that read "No Such Thing." He pondered this for a while then picked up a thin volume titled "Myths and Realities."

Curiosity piqued,
